context: Tests the agent's application of the tile's CSS-aware element-style-resolution pattern. The task says the three sub-components must be styleable three ways with a specific precedence order, but does not name `resolveEffectiveStyle`, the nullable-Style-field pattern, the DEFAULT_X_STYLE constant convention, the JavaDoc CSS-Child-Selectors heading, or any specific child-selector keyword — those choices are the tile's contribution.

checklist.name: Three nullable Style fields
checklist.description: The HistogramElement class declares three Style fields (one per sub-component: bar body, count label, axis label) with no inline initializer. Fields are NOT initialized to `Style.EMPTY` — that breaks CSS resolution because the explicit-vs-CSS check sees a non-null value.

checklist.name: Three DEFAULT_*_STYLE constants
checklist.description: Three `private static final Style DEFAULT_<NAME>_STYLE = ...` constants exist alongside the three nullable fields, one per sub-component. These are the fallback when both the explicit field and CSS produce nothing.

checklist.name: resolveEffectiveStyle in render path
checklist.description: The render method (e.g. `renderContent`) calls `resolveEffectiveStyle(context, "<child-name>", <field>, <DEFAULT>)` once per sub-component. Does NOT manually pick `field != null ? field : DEFAULT` — that bypasses the CSS layer of the precedence.

checklist.name: Toolkit factory method added
checklist.description: `Toolkit.java` gains a `public static HistogramElement histogram(...)` factory method matching the lower-camel-case convention used by the existing factories (`gauge`, `barChart`, `lineGauge`, etc.).

checklist.name: Fluent setters return this
checklist.description: Each style setter (e.g. `barStyle(Style)`, `labelStyle(Style)`, `axisStyle(Style)`) returns `this` so callers can chain — matches the rest of the toolkit DSL.

checklist.name: JavaDoc CSS Child Selectors block
checklist.description:
The HistogramElement class JavaDoc contains an HTML `

CSS Child Selectors

` heading followed by a list of every `HistogramElement-` selector with its default. Without this, a CSS-aware field is undocumented and unfindable.

checklist.name: AGENTS.md table updated
checklist.description: `AGENTS.md` gains exactly one new row in the existing 'Available CSS Child Selectors' table, listing the three child selector suffixes for HistogramElement. Does not duplicate or rewrite other rows.

checklist.name: Test asserts via BufferAssertions
checklist.description: The JUnit 5 test renders into a `Buffer` at a fixed `Rect` and asserts via `BufferAssertions` (the project's preferred test fixture) rather than ad-hoc `assertEquals` on cell-by-cell strings.

checklist.name: Java 8 source compatibility preserved
checklist.description: Library-side files (HistogramElement.java, Toolkit.java) avoid Java-9+ language features: no `var`, no text blocks, no records, no `List.of`, no pattern-matching `instanceof`/`switch`. The test file may use newer features.

checklist.name: Render-thread invariant respected
checklist.description: Any custom render helpers that mutate shared element state call `RenderThread.checkRenderThread()` at entry, OR rely on `StyledElement` superclass behavior (which already enforces this). Pure read-only render code is fine without an explicit check.
